entries.definition:
–ي
enquire; to be at ease; to be coy, to be coquettish; to act brazenly; a middleman. Of this root, two forms occur seven times in the Qurʾan: دَلَّ dalla six times and دَلِيل dalīl once.

دَلَّ dalla u [v. trans.] 1 to give a clue to, to indicate (34:14) مَا دَلَّهُمْ عَلَىٰ مَوْتِهِ إِلَّا دَابَّةُ الْأَرْضِ nothing indicated his death to them but the creature of the earth [said to be ‘the termite’] gnawing his staff 2 to point someone to, to show, to lead to (20:120) ءَادَمُ هَلْ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَىٰ شَجَرَةِ الْخُلْدِ وَمُلْكٍ لَّا يَبْلَىٰ Adam, shall I show you the tree of immortality and a kingdom that never wanes?

دَلِيل dalīl [n.] marker, pointer, indicator, proof, guide (25:45) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَيْفَ مَدَّ الظِّلَّ وَلَوْ شَاءَ لَجَعَلَهُ سَاكِنًا ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَا الشَّمْسَ عَلَيْهِ دَلِيلًا have you not considered how your Lord lengthens the shadow?, if He had willed, He could have made it still–then We would have made the sun its indicator.
entries.definition_english:
–ي
enquire; to be at ease; to be coy, to be coquettish; to act brazenly; a middleman. Of this root, two forms occur seven times in the Qurʾan: دَلَّ dalla six times and دَلِيل dalīl once.

دَلَّ dalla u [v. trans.] 1 to give a clue to, to indicate (34:14) مَا دَلَّهُمْ عَلَىٰ مَوْتِهِ إِلَّا دَابَّةُ الْأَرْضِ nothing indicated his death to them but the creature of the earth [said to be ‘the termite’] gnawing his staff 2 to point someone to, to show, to lead to (20:120) ءَادَمُ هَلْ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَىٰ شَجَرَةِ الْخُلْدِ وَمُلْكٍ لَّا يَبْلَىٰ Adam, shall I show you the tree of immortality and a kingdom that never wanes?

دَلِيل dalīl [n.] marker, pointer, indicator, proof, guide (25:45) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَيْفَ مَدَّ الظِّلَّ وَلَوْ شَاءَ لَجَعَلَهُ سَاكِنًا ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَا الشَّمْسَ عَلَيْهِ دَلِيلًا have you not considered how your Lord lengthens the shadow?, if He had willed, He could have made it still–then We would have made the sun its indicator.

entries.definition:
–ي d–l–w/y bucket, pail, to let down a bucket into a well; to hang down, to come near; to lead on, to tempt, to deceive; to submit a proof, to offer money as a bribe; to use one’s good offices. Of this root, four forms occur five times in the Qurʾan: دَلَّى dallā once; أَدْلَى ʾadlā twice; تَدَلَّى tadallā once and دَلْو dalw once.
دَلَّى dallā [v. II, trans.] to lead someone on, to lure (7:22) فَدَلَّاهُمَا بِغُرُورٍ so, he lured them with guile, and when they tasted the tree their shameful parts became manifest to them.

أَدْلَى ʾadlā [v. IV, trans.] 1 to let down (12:19) وَجَاءَتْ سَيَّارَةٌ فَأَرْسَلُوا وَارِدَهُمْ فَأَدْلَىٰ دَلْوَهُ then came travellers, and they sent their water fetcher, and he let down his bucket 2 [with prep. بِـ] to offer or present [money] to [someone in authority] as a bribe] (2:188) وَلَا تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوَالَكُم بَيْنَكُم بِالْبَاطِلِ وَتُدْلُوا بِهَا إِلَى الْحُكَّامِ لِتَأْكُلُوا فَرِيقًا مِّنْ أَمْوَالِ النَّاسِ بِالْإِثْمِ do not appropriate each other’s property in dealings together in a corrupt way, nor use your property to bribe judges/rulers, intending sinfully and knowingly to appropriate [even] a part of other people’s property.

تَدَلَّى tadallā [v. V, intrans.] to hang down, to approach closely, to draw near (53:7–8) فَكَانَ قَابَ قَوْسَيْنِ أَوْ أَدْنَىٰ and then he

313
